scheme_name: ICAR - Post Matric Scholarship For Scheduled Caste / Scheduled Tribes Candidates
page_title: ICAR - Post Matric Scholarship For Scheduled Caste / Scheduled Tribes Candidates

details: The scheme "Post-Matric Scholarship to Scheduled Castes / Scheduled Tribes Candidates" was introduced by the Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR), Department of Agricultural Research and Education (DARE), to enable the students belonging to the weaker sections of society to complete undergraduate courses. The scholarship is provided for studying at the Bachelor's Degree program in various branches of Agriculture viz. B.Sc.(Agri.), B.V.Sc., B.Sc.(Agril. Engg.), B.Sc.(Home Science), B.F.Sc., B.Sc.(Forestry) etc. The value of the scholarship will be ₹ 1,000/- per month along with a contingent grant of ₹ 750/- per annum for the payment of tuition fees, purchase of books, cost of uniforms, etc. The duration of the scholarship will cover the whole of the normal period of the particular degree program to which the candidate is admitted.
benefits: The value of the scholarship will be ₹ 1,000/- per month along with a contingent grant of ₹ 750/- per annum for the payment of tuition fees, purchase of books, cost of uniforms, etc.
The duration of the scholarship will cover the whole of the normal period of the particular degree program to which the candidate is admitted.
eligibility: The applicant must be a citizen of India.
The applicant must belong to Scheduled Castes / Scheduled Tribes communities as defined by the Government of India.

The applicant must have been admitted to Agricultural Universities in any of the Agriculture & Allied Sciences undergraduate degree programs, viz. B.Sc.(Agri.), B.V.Sc., B.Sc.(Agril. Engg.), B.Sc.(Home Science), B.F.Sc., B.Sc.(Forestry) etc.

offline: The scholarship amount as well as the contingent grant will be remitted to the respective institutions by the Council based on the demand received from each institution through the Registrar / Comptroller of the University in the prescribed
The students will be eligible to receive scholarships and contingent grants from the date of joining the course.
1. A tentative distribution of scholarships will first be made to various agricultural universities on the basis of seats reserved by the individual agricultural universities for SC/ST candidates.

2. The actual number of scholarships allotted to the universities every year would, however, be determined on the basis of actual SC/ST students admitted so as to maintain approximately equal proportion between the number of SC/ST students admitted in each university to the number of scholarships available.

documents_required: Details of the Aadhaar Linked Bank Account.
Marksheets of the Previous Qualified Examination.

faqs.question: What is the Eligibility Criteria for this scholarship?
faqs.answer: This scholarship is exclusively meant for students belonging to Scheduled Castes / Scheduled Tribes communities as defined by Government of India. Only those students who have been admitted in Agricultural Universities in any of the undergraduate degree programmes of Agriculture & Allied Sciences will be eligible for this scholarship.

faqs.question: What is the Value Of The Scholarship?
faqs.answer: The value of the scholarship will be Rs.300/- p.m. along with a contingent grant of Rs.750/- per annum for the payment of tuition fee, purchase of books and cost of uniform etc.

faqs.question: What Is The Duration Of The Scholarship?
faqs.answer: The duration of the scholarship will cover the whole of the normal period of the particular degree programme to which the candidate is admitted.

faqs.question: What is the Procedure for Selection?
faqs.answer: The scheme provides for the award of 240 scholarships every year. These scholarships will be earmarked for individual Agricultural Universities in proportion to the actual number of scheduled castes / scheduled tribes candidates admitted. A tentative distribution of scholarships will first be made to various agricultural universities on the basis of seats reserved by the individual agricultural universities for SC/ST candidates. The actual number of scholarship allotted to the universities every year would, however, be determined on the basis of actual SC/ST students admitted so as to maintain approximately equal proportion between the number of SC/ST students admitted in each university to the number of scholarships available.

faqs.question: Will the Student Who Accepts This Scholarship Be Permitted To Avail Of Any Other Scholarship/Concession?
faqs.answer: The student who accepts this scholarship will not be permitted to avail of any other scholarship/concession provided by any other agencies nor permitted to accept any part-time employment.

faqs.question: Under what cases can the Scholarship of the student be Terminated?
faqs.answer: Termination of Scholarship:- The scholarship will be terminated without notice (i) from the date the scholar debases to be on the roll of the institution or on the date of expiry of the sanction of the scholarship whichever is earlier. The scholarship can also be terminated without notice at any time if in the opinion of the institution and the Council the scholar is found to be negligent in his/her studies or is guilty of misconduct; (ii) if the scholar is dropped by the institution due to peer performance.

faqs.question: Will the Scholarship Be Admissible On Grounds Of Maternity?
faqs.answer: In the case of married women, the scholarship will be admissible at full rates upto three months' absence on grounds of maternity. However, no scholarship will be paid for a period exceeding three months.

faqs.question: Which branches of Agriculture of the Bachelor's Degree Program are covered in this scheme?
faqs.answer: Bachelor's Degree program in various branches of Agriculture, viz. B.Sc.(Agri.), B.V.Sc., B.Sc.(Agril. Engg.), B.Sc.(Home Science), B.F.Sc., B.Sc.(Forestry) etc.

faqs.question: How many scholarships are awarded every year?
faqs.answer: The scheme provides for the award of 240 scholarships every year.

faqs.question: Is there any earmarking of the scholarships?
faqs.answer: These scholarships will be earmarked for individual Agricultural Universities in proportion to the actual number of scheduled castes / scheduled tribes candidates admitted.
